plugin_desc: Ruijie (锐捷) switch-specific SNMP collection (RUIJIE-PROCESS/MEMORY/SYSTEM MIBs, IANA PEN 4881). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Ruijie private OIDs for the full device-health set: CPU utilization (ruijieNodeCPUTotal5min), system memory (size/used, KB), chassis temperature (ruijieSystemMultipleTemperatureCurrent), fan state (ruijieSystemFanStatus) and power-supply state (ruijieSystemElectricalInformationStatus). Fan/PSU raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. Memory utilization is computed from used/total. Supported brands/models — Ruijie Networks RG-series switches.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Ruijie device control plane (RUIJIE-PROCESS-MIB ruijieNodeCPUTotal5min, five-minute average), averaged across nodes and normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the Ruijie system memory pool (RUIJIE-MEMORY-MIB ruijieMemoryPoolSize, reported in KB and scaled to bytes). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently in use by the Ruijie system memory pool (RUIJIE-MEMORY-MIB ruijieMemoryPoolUsed, reported in KB and scaled to bytes). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Ruijie device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Ruijie device (RUIJIE-SYSTEM-MIB ruijieSystemMultipleTemperatureCurrent, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across sensors. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan state of the Ruijie device (RUIJIE-SYSTEM-MIB ruijieSystemFanStatus), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy (not-present / ready / normal) and 2 = fault (no-power / abnormal / unknown). Aggregated as the maximum across fans so any faulty fan surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a fan failure that can lead to overheating.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply state of the Ruijie device (RUIJIE-SYSTEM-MIB ruijieSystemElectricalInformationStatus), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy (not-present / ready / normal) and 2 = fault (no-power / abnormal / unknown). Aggregated as the maximum across supplies so any failed PSU surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply problem.

metrics.name: device_fan_speed
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan rotation speed in RPM of the Ruijie device (RUIJIE-SYSTEM-MIB ruijieSystemFanStatusSpeed), taken as the maximum across fans. Abnormally low or zero RPM indicates a stalled/failed fan and impending overheating; abnormally high RPM can indicate the device is compensating for high temperature.

metrics.name: device_voltage_volts
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply input voltage in volts of the Ruijie device (RUIJIE-SYSTEM-MIB ruijieSystemElectricalInformationInVoltage), taken as the maximum across supplies so the live PSU's mains voltage is reflected (absent supplies report 0). A reading far from the nominal mains voltage indicates a power-input problem (brownout, wiring, or a failing PSU).

metrics.name: device_optical_rx_power
metrics.description: This metric reports the weakest SFP/optical receive power across the device's fiber ports, in dBm (RUIJIE-FIBER-MIB ruijieFiberRXpower, raw value scaled by 1/100; the -10000 'no module' sentinel is filtered out). Taken as the minimum (weakest signal) per device because a dropping RX level is the earliest sign of a degrading or dirty optic/fiber. Typical good RX is roughly -3 to -15 dBm; values approaching the receiver sensitivity floor risk link errors.

metrics.name: device_optical_tx_power
metrics.description: This metric reports the weakest SFP/optical transmit power across the device's fiber ports, in dBm (RUIJIE-FIBER-MIB ruijieFiberTXpower, raw value scaled by 1/100; the -10000 'no module' sentinel is filtered out). Taken as the minimum per device; an abnormally low TX level indicates a failing laser/optic that will soon drop the link.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
